summary: Wire per-parent deletion reconciliation into dependent sync. After a --full sync fully enumerates a per_parent dependent partition (e.g. cycles/modules/labels/states/archived_*/intake_issues/projects_issues scoped by $.project), prune local rows the API no longer returns for that parent. store.go gains ReconcilePartition (mark-and-sweep over the typed table + CascadeJunctionsFor junction cascades); sync.go threads a prune flag (full && !no-prune), tracks per-partition completeness via partitionOutcome (complete=true only at the three natural-enumeration ends, never on error/cap/cursor exits), accumulates seenIDs before the completeness breaks, and calls ReconcilePartition under the prune && complete && per_parent guard, emitting reconcile / reconcile_error / reconcile_skipped events. A proven-complete sweep always emits a reconcile event (with deleted:0 when the cache was already consistent) so a clean run stays observable rather than silent. A new --no-prune flag opts out. modules_novel.go registers the module-issues junction cascade (for both 'modules' and 'archived_modules') so reconcile deletes flow through novel membership tables.
reason: A full resync that re-enumerates a parent's children should converge local state to the API: rows deleted upstream must disappear locally, not linger. Without reconciliation the local SQLite store accumulates orphaned children forever. Reconcile is gated to fully-enumerated per_parent partitions so a partial/errored enumeration can never trigger a destructive delete.
validated_outcome: go build ./..., go vet ./internal/..., and go test ./internal/store/ ./internal/cli/ all pass (incl. reconcile_test.go: TestReconcilePartition_SweepsDeletedKeepsSeenAndOtherPartitions, TestReconcilePartition_EmptyScopeErrors). outcome.complete=true appears at exactly 3 sites (legit-empty isEmptyPageResponse branch, !resourceSupportsPagination break, !hasMore||short break); seenIDs accumulation runs before both complete-breaks; no error/cap/cursor exit sets complete.

deferred_to_upstream.feature: Composite-key per_parent reconcile (storageID != id) and two-path-param nested dependent reconcile.
deferred_to_upstream.reason: seenIDs assumes storageID==id, true only for single-path-param per_parent resources; nested two-path-param dependents are marked ReconcileMode:none this round.
